sense.definition: a long and thin sports tool used for hitting a ball in games like baseball, cricket, or tennis

examples.x: He swung the bat and hit a home run.
The cricket player chose a new bat for the match.
She practiced her hits with a bat.
The coach showed him the proper way to grip the bat.
The old bat had a few dents from years of use.

sense.definition: a sports equipment used in field hockey, consisting of a long, slender shaft with a curved head for striking the ball

examples.x: The field hockey stick broke during the intense match.
She bought a new field hockey stick for the upcoming season.
He practiced dribbling with his field hockey stick every afternoon.
The team captain personalized her field hockey stick with bright colors.
The player swung her field hockey stick with precision and scored a goal.

sense.definition: a sports tool with a netted pouch on one end for playing lacrosse

examples.x: His lacrosse stick is custom-made.
She practices with her lacrosse stick every day.
The lacrosse stick's head broke during the game.
He carries his lacrosse stick in a special bag.
The lacrosse stick's shaft is lightweight.

sense.definition: a sports equipment with a handle and a netted frame, used by players to hit the tennis ball

examples.x: She swung her tennis racket to serve the ball across the net.
The coach handed him a new tennis racket before the big game.
The professional player signed his old tennis racket and gave it to a fan.
His tennis racket was customized with his initials.
She practiced serving with her new tennis racket every day.

sense.definition: a tool used to move a boat or raft through the water, consisting of a long handle with a flat or curved blade on one end

examples.x: She grabbed the paddle and began rowing across the lake.
The paddle splashed loudly as he dipped it into the water.
They used long wooden paddles to row the boat down the stream.
The boat’s paddle broke, leaving them stranded in the middle of the lake.
The paddles on the raft helped us navigate through the river's current.

sense.definition: a sports tool used by players to hit the shuttlecock in badminton matches

examples.x: She practiced serving with her new badminton racket every day.
The team packed extra badminton rackets for the tournament.
She accidentally broke her badminton racket during a heated match.
He forgot his badminton racket at home and had to borrow one from a friend.
The coach handed her a new badminton racket before the big game.

sense.definition: a long, spear-like tool used by athletes to throw as far as possible in track and field events

examples.x: The athlete gripped the javelin tightly before his throw.
The coach gave him tips on how to hold the javelin correctly.
She carried her javelin to the field for the training session.
She practiced her javelin technique every day to improve her distance.
He chose a lightweight javelin for better control and speed.

sense.definition: a type of golf club used by players to hit the ball long distances from the tee

examples.x: He used his driver to hit the ball down the fairway.
She practiced her swing with the driver to improve her distance.
The coach recommended a new driver to enhance his game.
He kept his favorite driver in perfect condition for tournaments.
She felt confident holding her driver on the first tee.

sense.definition: a tool used by golfers to hit the ball in various ways during a round of golf

examples.x: He chose a golf club from his bag to make the perfect shot.
She practiced her swing with each golf club to improve her game.
She used a different golf club for putting than for driving.
His favorite golf club was a seven iron because of its versatility.
The golfer inspected his golf club for any damage before the tournament.

sense.definition: an object with a handle, an oval frame and a tightly fixed net, used for hitting the ball in sports such as badminton, tennis, etc.

examples.x: She swung her racket with precision to win the tennis match.
The badminton player chose a lightweight racket for better control.
His racket broke after a powerful serve during the game.
They bought new rackets for their weekend tennis practice.
The professional player autographed a racket for his fan.

sense.definition: a tool with a large, usually wooden, head used for striking objects, often used in sports like polo or croquet

examples.x: The player swung the mallet to hit the ball through the hoop.
The coach showed him the proper way to hold the mallet.
She gripped the mallet tightly, ready to take her shot in croquet.
His mallet broke during the intense polo match.
The player adjusted the mallet's handle for a better grip.

sense.definition: a tool used by rowers to propel and steer a boat through the water

examples.x: She pulled the oar through the water with a strong, steady stroke.
He adjusted his grip on the oar for better control.
She lost her balance and almost dropped the oar overboard.
He carved his initials into the wooden handle of his oar.

sense.definition: a sport equipment used by players to hit, pass, and control the puck or ball in hockey games

examples.x: She practiced her slapshot with her new hockey stick.
He used the hockey stick to pass the puck to his teammate.
The hockey stick felt balanced in his hands.
She bought a lightweight hockey stick for better maneuverability.
The coach showed him how to properly handle the hockey stick.

sense.definition: a sports equipment used by skiers to assist with balance and propulsion on snow

examples.x: He planted his ski pole firmly in the snow to make a sharp turn.
She chose a lightweight ski pole for better control on the mountain.
She decorated her ski pole with colorful stickers.
The skier relied on his ski pole for stability on the steep hill.
He used the ski pole to push himself forward on the flat terrain.

sense.definition: a tall, vertical pole on a ship or boat that supports the sails and rigging

examples.x: The sailor climbed the mast to adjust the sails.
The ship's mast stood tall against the sky.
They raised the flag to the top of the mast.
The wind filled the sails attached to the mast.
He secured the ropes tightly to the mast.

sense.definition: a type of sports equipment used in bandy, resembling a hockey stick but with a curved blade

examples.x: He swung the bandy stick and scored a goal.
The bandy stick broke during the match.
She practiced her dribbling skills with the bandy stick.
The goalie deflected the shot with his bandy stick.
Players need to be quick on their feet when using a bandy stick.

sense.definition: a type of sword used in the sport of fencing, characterized by its flexible blade and specific rules for scoring points

examples.x: The fencer's foil glinted under the arena lights.
She adjusted her grip on the foil before the match began.
He practiced his parries and lunges with the foil.
She fenced competitively with a lightweight foil.
He aimed carefully before thrusting with the foil.

sense.definition: a weapon characterized by a three-sided blade and a bell guard, used in competitive fencing where the entire body is a valid target area for scoring points

examples.x: The fencer adjusted her grip on the epee to improve her point control.
The epee blade's flexibility allows for precise attacks and defensive actions.
The flexibility of the epee blade allows for both defensive blocks and offensive strikes.
Using an epee requires precise handling and control to land accurate strikes.
Her opponent defended against the epee's thrusts with quick parries and counters.

sense.definition: a type of sword used in fencing, characterized by its slightly curved blade and specific rules for scoring points in saber fencing

examples.x: He trained diligently with the saber to improve his technique.
The fencer's saber gleamed in the sunlight as he prepared to compete.
She adjusted the grip on her saber for better control.
She parried her opponent's saber attack with precision.
He swung his saber in a swift, decisive motion.

sense.definition: a traditional Japanese long staff used in martial arts such as Aikido and Karate

examples.x: He practiced his strikes with the bo every morning.
The bo is a versatile weapon, used for both offense and defense.
She demonstrated a series of intricate spins with the bo.
The bo is typically made from hardwood or bamboo.
Students were instructed in the proper grip of the bo.

sense.definition: a pair of wooden or metal sticks connected by a chain or rope, typically used in martial arts such as karate and taekwondo

examples.x: The martial artist swung the nunchaku with precision and grace.
She demonstrated her skill with the nunchaku during the tournament.
Training with nunchaku requires focus and discipline.
He accidentally hit himself on the head while learning nunchaku moves.

sense.definition: a traditional martial arts weapon with a metal shaft and two prongs used in karate

examples.x: The sai was used for blocking and striking in the demonstration.
She learned how to handle the sai safely.
They studied the history of the sai in martial arts.
She carried her sai in a protective case.

sense.definition: a type of weapon consisting of a long metal blade and a handle

examples.x: The knight drew his sword from its sheath, ready to defend the castle.
He admired the ancient samurai sword displayed in the museum.
The pirate brandished his sword menacingly as he approached his opponent.
Swords were commonly used in medieval duels and battles.

sense.definition: a weapon with a long handle and a metal pointed tip, used for fighting and fishing in the past

examples.x: The hunter used a spear to catch fish in the river.
Warriors in ancient Greece wielded spears and shields in battle.
He crafted a spear from a sturdy tree branch and a sharpened stone for the tip.
The museum displayed a collection of spears used by indigenous tribes for hunting and defense.
During the hunt, the tribesmen worked together to surround the wild boar and attack it with spears.

sense.definition: a wooden Japanese sword used in martial arts training, such as kenjutsu and aikido

examples.x: The students sparred with bokkens during their aikido class.
The dojo displayed a variety of beautifully crafted bokkens on the wall.
She carefully wrapped her bokken in a cloth after training.
His bokken technique improved after months of rigorous practice.

sense.definition: a bamboo sword used in Japanese martial arts, particularly in kendo

examples.x: He practiced swinging the shinai with precision.
The students lined up to receive their shinai for practice.
The dojo stored dozens of shinai for its members.
The shinai's weight was perfectly balanced for swift strikes.

sense.definition: a curved weapon joined at both ends by a string, capable of shooting arrows

examples.x: The archer strung his bow and notched an arrow, aiming carefully at the distant target.
Her ancient bow, handed down through generations, was still as strong and flexible as ever.
He practiced for hours, perfecting his technique with the new compound bow he had purchased.
The warrior's bow was intricately carved, with symbols of protection etched along its length.

sense.definition: a type of weapon consisting of a metal or wooden bar with a sharp head and feathers at the end

examples.x: The archer drew his bow and released the arrow, hitting the target with precision.
The hunter nocked an arrow and aimed carefully at the deer.
In medieval times, arrows were crucial weapons used by soldiers in battle.

sense.definition: the cord or string that connects the two ends of a bow, used to propel arrows in archery

examples.x: He carefully inspected the bowstring for any signs of wear before shooting.
The archer adjusted the tension of the bowstring for better accuracy.
The archer's fingers grazed the bowstring as he drew the bow.
His bowstring snapped just as he released the arrow.
She replaced the old bowstring with a new one for the upcoming tournament.

sense.definition: the feathers or vanes on an arrow that stabilize its flight, commonly used in archery and hunting

examples.x: He adjusted the angle of the fletching to compensate for the wind.
The fletching on his arrows was brightly colored for visibility during competitions.
The archer carefully attached the fletching to each arrow for optimal accuracy.
She noticed the fletching on her arrows was worn and needed replacement.
The traditional arrows had fletching made from turkey feathers.

sense.definition: (golf) a part of the clubhead, specifically the area closest to the shaft

examples.x: He struck the ball off the heel of the club, causing it to veer off course.
The golfer adjusted his stance to avoid hitting the ball with the heel.
She felt a slight vibration in her hands when she hit the ball with the heel.
The heel of his club showed signs of wear from frequent use.
He aimed carefully to prevent hitting the ball with the heel.

sense.definition: a traditional Japanese bow used in kyudo

examples.x: He practiced with his yumi every morning to improve his archery skills.
His yumi was handed down through generations of his family.
The yumi was beautifully crafted from bamboo and leather.
The yumi's grip was wrapped in soft deer hide for comfort.
He learned to string his yumi with precision and care.

sense.definition: a long gun suitable for shooting a target over long distances, which is held along shoulder while aiming the target

examples.x: He adjusted the scope on his rifle before taking aim at the distant target.
Hunters often prefer rifles for their accuracy and range when tracking game in the wilderness.
The soldier carried a lightweight rifle as part of his standard equipment during patrols.
She learned to shoot a rifle at a young age, honing her marksmanship skills at the shooting range.
The museum displayed historical rifles used by soldiers throughout different periods of warfare.

sense.definition: a handheld firearm typically used for personal defense or target shooting

examples.x: He trained at the shooting range with his pistol.
The detective carried a concealed pistol for protection.
She aimed her pistol at the target with steady hands.
His pistol was loaded with ammunition for the competition.
The police officer holstered her pistol after apprehending the suspect.

sense.definition: a sports tool used by players in the game of squash to hit the ball against the walls of the court

examples.x: She adjusted the tension of the strings on her squash racket.
She practiced her backhand shots with her new squash racket.
His old squash racket had a worn grip from years of use.
The coach advised him to grip the squash racket firmly during swings.
